title:
Lesson 1:
It's Hot
description: 'Hace calor' = It's hot (weather). '¡Qué calor!' = How hot! / It's so hot! (exclamation). 'Está caliente' = It's hot (for objects or food, not weather). Note this key difference.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: How hot! I can't go out like this.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Sí, hace mucho calor hoy. Son cuarenta grados.
dialogue.english: Yes, it's very hot today. It's forty degrees.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: No gracias, está muy caliente. Prefiero agua fría.
dialogue.english: No thanks, it's too hot. I prefer cold water.

title:
Lesson 2:
It's Cold / I'm Cold
description: 'Hace frío' = It's cold (weather). '¡Qué frío!' = How cold! / It's so cold! 'Tengo frío' = I'm cold (literally: I have cold). Use 'tengo frío' for your own feeling, 'hace frío' for the weather.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, it's very cold. Are you cold?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I'm cold. I forgot my jacket.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Toma el mío. No me importa.
